title: Lepidaploa pseudaurea
intro: Lepidaploa pseudaurea, also known as False Aurea, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lepidaploa pseudaurea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, and is also used in folk medicine to make topical creams and ointments.

The genus Lepidaploa is a part of the tribe Vernonieae and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. Lepidaploa pseudaurea is a member of the infrageneric group known as Lepidaploa, which is characterized by its distinctive flower shape and arrangement. Lepidaploa pseudaurea's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Asteraceae family.
